explanation: The exact premises establish that SCPs do not constrain management-account identities and that some services support delegated administration. They do not themselves establish the privileged-resource exposure or AWS guidance to keep ordinary workloads/resources out of the management account. The direct guidance citation cannot replace an atomic premise claim.
required_action: Add an exact premise claim for the management-account workload/resource isolation guidance (and its rationale), then reverify the derivation.
suggested_revision: Reference an atomic approved premise stating that AWS recommends limiting management-account resources and data to functions that must be managed there because privileged operations are available and SCPs do not apply.

supporting_source.locator: Use the management account only for tasks that require the management account; Avoid deploying workloads to the organization’s management account; Delegate responsibilities outside the management account for decentralization
supporting_source.title: Best practices for the management account
